name: Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular
name_original: Picacho, Sant Juri, La Vella y Puntal de Matamoros — circular desde La Cuesta, Hondón de las Nieves
region: El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves
description: Hike the difficult 9.3-mile Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular, gaining 2020 feet with panoramic views and multiple summits.
region_intro: Hiking around El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves offers diverse natural landscapes within the Hondon Valley of Alicante, Spain. The region features rugged mountains, serene valleys, and unique geological formations like canyons and karst landscapes. It is characterized by the Sierra de Crevillent and Sierra de la Quibas, alongside ancient olive groves, vineyards, and almond trees. The area provides varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular, a difficult 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route provides panoramic views across multiple summits. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico de Sant Gaita – Alt de Sant Juri Summit loop from Merendero de Hondon de las Nieves, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This loop offers views of mountain peaks and the surrounding valley. Local hikers also love the Pico La Vella – La Vella Antennas loop from el Fondó de les Neus / Hondón de las Nieves, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with antennas, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves is defined by rugged mountains, serene valleys, and ancient olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore the diverse landscapes of the Hondon Valley.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves offers 5 easy hiking trails. These routes are perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll through the scenic valleys, often passing through ancient olive groves and vineyards.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in this region?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, from rugged mountains like the Sierra de Crevillent and Sierra de la Quibas to serene valleys filled with olive, almond, and Monastrell grape groves. You'll encounter varied terrain including mountain paths, valley trails, and unique geological formations such as canyons and karst landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316989212/sierra-de-crevillent-peaks-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6891461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Sant Gaita – Alt de Sant Juri Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic loop from Merendero de Hondon de las Nieves.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover several natural highlights. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Waterfall of the Via Ferrata&lt;/a&gt;, cross the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinalopó River&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the geological formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rambla Salada&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also rich in mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3782652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de Sant Juri Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Many trails lead to elevated points with expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316989212/sierra-de-crevillent-peaks-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for its panoramic vistas across multiple summits. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Vella – La Vella Antennas loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7101299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vella Antennas&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful when the valley transforms with wild flowers, poppies, and aromatic herbs. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: While specific family-designated trails aren't listed, the 5 easy routes available in the region are generally suitable for families with children. These trails typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed outdoor experience amidst the natural beauty of the Hondon Valley.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves?
A: Many trails in natural areas like El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves are generally dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. Given the diverse flora and fauna, it's always recommended to keep pets under control to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves?
A: The hiking routes in El Fondó De Les Neus / Hondón De Las Nieves are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic views from the peaks, and the serene beauty of the valleys with their ancient groves.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, there are 4 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316989212/sierra-de-crevillent-peaks-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.3-mile trail that rewards with extensive views across multiple summits. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33814577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuesta Recreation Area – Alt de Sant Juri Summit loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering a longer and more strenuous adventure.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the hiking trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes, especially those starting from villages like Hondón de las Nieves or recreation areas, typically have designated parking spots. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking at the starting point of your chosen hike.
tips: Recreational area with benches and tables to barbecue and spend the day. The surroundings are very beautiful, many trees, pines, almond trees, aromatic herbs.
The peak of Picacho (Picatxo in Valencian or the peak of San Cayetano) offers us magnificent views of the entire Sierra de Crevillente.
The Alt de Sant Juri is 812 meters high. This peak offers magnificent views of the great rocky walls that rise above the Anouers ravine.

The La Vella peak is part of the Sierra de Crevillent and offers wonderful views. You reach the top along the marked path in good condition and without too much technical difficulty. It is recommended to bring plenty of water because there are no sources on the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this circular route is varied and can be challenging. You'll encounter a mix of hiking paths, mountain hiking paths, and unpaved sections. Expect abrupt and steep ascents, particularly to peaks, with some areas featuring somewhat decomposed soil and loose rocks. The Sierra is also characterized by barrancos (ravines), fallas (faults), cuevas (caves), and ramblas (dry riverbeds).

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult. It involves significant elevation gain and varied, challenging terrain, including steep and rocky sections. It requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness, making it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular trail?
faqs.answer: A common starting point for trails in the Sierra de Crevillent, and likely for this route, is near the 'Els Pontets' aqueduct. You can also consider starting from the La Cuesta Recreation Area, which is a highlight along the route and often has parking facilities.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the specific trailheads in the Sierra de Crevillent can be limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the starting points, such as the 'Els Pontets' aqueduct or the La Cuesta Recreation Area.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for hiking in the Sierra de Crevillent?
faqs.answer: No specific hiking permits or entrance fees are generally required to access the trails in the Sierra de Crevillent. The area is part of the European Natura 2000 network, designated as a Special Conservation Zone (ZEC) and a Site of Community Importance (LIC), so please respect the natural environment and follow all local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot in this region, making the difficult ascent more challenging. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and potentially more greenery, while autumn provides comfortable hiking conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers spectacular panoramic views. You'll pass several significant peaks, including the Summit of Picacho, Alt de Sant Juri Summit, Pico La Vella (the highest point of the sierra), and El Puntal de Matamoros. From various vantage points, you can see the Bajo Vinalopó, parts of the Vega Baja, the Crevillente reservoir, the Natural Park of El Hondo, and even the Mediterranean Sea. You might also pass the Frogs' Pool.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Crevillent Peaks Circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Crevillent is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed. Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural parks, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails in the area. You will find yourself on parts of the L'alt pelat, Sierra de Crevillente, and Els Anouers trails, among others, as you navigate the Sierra.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no natural water sources or facilities to refill water bottles directly along this trail. It is highly recommended to carry plenty of water, especially given the trail's difficulty and the potential for warm weather.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring ample water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good grip, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable due to the varied terrain and intersecting paths.
